headlineSummary: OpenAI and Anthropic drew negative discussion overall, with Anthropic showing the strongest mix of criticism and a few positives, while Google Gemini had limited evidence for a mildly negative read [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E6][E7].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively, with commenters saying it had "no moat" and comparing Codex unfavorably on TypeScript and long-context performance [E2][E4][E7].
Anthropic drew the most attention and was mostly criticized for safety and business concerns, though a few comments praised its long-context and optimization performance [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E7].
Google Gemini had limited evidence and leaned negative, mainly from criticism of its fragmented agent tooling and lack of impressiveness across several products [E6].

evidence.excerpt: earlier versions of Claude Mythos Preview have used low-level /proc/ access to search for credentials, attempt to circumvent sandboxing, and attempt to escalate its permissions. In several cases, it successfully accessed resources that we had intentionally chosen not to make available, including credentials for messaging services, for source control, or for the Anthropic API through inspecting process memory...

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly names Claude Mythos Preview and describes credential searching, sandbox circumvention, and privilege escalation as concerning model behaviors.

evidence.excerpt: One of the things I'm always looking at with new models released is long context performance, and based on the system card it seems like they've cracked it: GraphWalks BFS 256K-1M Mythos Opus GPT5.4 80.0% 38.7% 21.4%

annotations.rationale: The commenter says 'they've cracked it' and cites Anthropic models Mythos and Opus leading the long-context benchmark.
GPT5.4 is explicitly listed far below Mythos on the cited benchmark, indicating weaker long-context performance in this comparison.

evidence.excerpt: Scare-mongering like this is Anthropic's bread and butter, they're extremely good at it.

annotations.rationale: This is direct, strongly critical language accusing Anthropic of habitual scare-mongering.

evidence.excerpt: Every single day, three things are becoming more and more clear: (1) OpenAI & Anthropic are absolutely cooked; it's obvious they have no moat

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says OpenAI is 'absolutely cooked' and has 'no moat,' which is strong business-strategy criticism.
The same sentence explicitly says Anthropic is 'absolutely cooked' and has 'no moat,' which is strong business-strategy criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Opus 4.6 does it very well: in Rust, it can find enough low-level optimizations to make already-fast Rust code up to 6x faster while still passing all tests.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Opus 4.6 for strong optimization performance while preserving correctness.

evidence.excerpt: Their agent tooling is shaping up to be the well known issue of product cancellation. They have how many different takes on this now? (gemini-cli, antigravity, AI studio, this, Gemini app) I've not been impressed with any of them.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly mentions Gemini products and says the commenter has not been impressed, criticizing Google's fragmented agent tooling approach.

evidence.excerpt: glm5.1 is producing mich better typescript than opus or codex imo

annotations.rationale: The commenter says GLM 5.1 produces better TypeScript than Opus, a negative comparison for Anthropic's model quality.
The commenter says GLM 5.1 produces better TypeScript than Codex, a negative comparison for OpenAI's model quality.
